calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-30-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Balin for Ann Cazahous. Philip Cazahous also present.

Mr. Balin reported that the parties have bene working together and cooperating, that there are 3 outstanding issues, that all documents have been submitted by Ann & Philip to Ameritrade but that John Jr. has not submitted his documents, that in regards to the Exon Mobil stock the paperwork is complete and they are just waiting for a check, that there are two accounts at Chase Bank, that the bank will only talk to Ann, that the hotel was sold and the transfer complete, that there was an order issued in 2014 requiring the funds to be placed into a blocked account, that Philip Cazahous has removed his objection and they ask to be able to distribute those funds at this time and that they filed a motion to seal documents which was denied by Judge Busch. The Court turned to the accounting and asked counsel if they were asking for it to be approved or allowed and Mr. Balin responded that they request approval. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Court Records
Off Calendar Mar-16-2016 Denied

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal Court Records
Off Calendar Mar-10-2016 Continued To Mar-16-16 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-25-2016 Continued To Mar-28-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Balin for Ann Cazahous. Matthew Mraule for and with Philip Cazahous.

The Court asked what are we trying to accomplish. Mr. Balin stated that Mr. Cazahous has objections, that in regards to the TD Ameritrade accounts communication was sent to previous counsel stating letters were needed from the 3 beneficiaries, that he has been told that Philip Cazahous did not sign his letter, that he has not received anything from John Cazahous, that in regards to the Exxon Mobil stock the company directed the trustee to open an account so that they could direct the money into it but that the money has not yet been deposited, that Chase Bank was asked long ago to distribute the funds from the two accounts but they have not yet done so, that they are currently in arbitration with Mr. Patel regarding the hotel, that tomorrow is phase 2, that the Judge ordered the sale of the hotel but Mr. Patel is objecting to the terms, that they expect the hotel to be sold in February or that it will remain in the trust, that they have a hearing date for their motion to seal records and that it has been served but was rejected when they attempted to file it, that they have kept Mr. Mraule informed, that in regards to the taxes he was just informed that the 706 has been received and that the accountant cannot issue the K-1 form yet and he does not have the information. Mr. Mraule stated that it is good to know about the tax return, that it may affect the terms of the settlement, that they had asked for information regarding the letters but received no response and that TD Ameritrade says they have everything. The court stated that this is either under submission or asked how they want to proceed and that the court cannot babysit the settlement agreement. Mr. Mraule stated that the settlement agreement only asks the court to accept the accounting, that they just want to get this done and requests that it only be continued for one month. Mr. Balin stated that the key item is the Chase account, asks that the matter be continued to late March or early April, that with the arbitration coming to an end he will be able to devote full time to this matter, that the trustee wants this done as well but one month is too soon. The Court asked for a recommendation if this is not complete by late March. Mr. Mraule stated that he is fine with late March but that if it is not complete at that time they would like a successor appointed. Mr. Balin stated that if they are back in late March they will request that the court approve the accounting, that they will close out the trust and that non trust items will be dealt with separately. The accounting petition was continued to March 28, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-02-2015 Continued To Jan-25-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Balin for (successor trustee) Ann Cazahous. Matt Mraule for Philip Cazahous.

The Court asked if the parties have the requested order or if they want to address the issues. Mr. Mraule stated that they had agreed to withdraw their objections with compliance by the trustee but that they are not accepting the accounting until then. Mr. Balin stated that there are 3 accounts that are not part of the trust which are to be distributed, that they are Exxon, TD Ameritrade and a bank account, that there is a delay in clearing the transfers of money/stocks, that it should be completed by the end of the year, that if there are objections they could be brought up later and that the accounting would not contain those accounts. Mr. Mraule stated that per the settlement agreement they are not accepting the accounting until everything is complied with. Mr. Balin stated that the pending matter of the dispute over the hotel has been bifurcated and will be heard in January and suggested a continuance until that time. Mr. Mraule stated that the K-1 will be due and that he has concerns. Mr. Balin stated that he will notify Ms. Verzosa as soon as something happens. The accounting petition was continued to January 25, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Sep-16-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mraule for Philip Cazahous. Dan Serot for (objector) Dilip Kumar Patel. William Balin for (trustee) Ann Cazahous.

Mr. Balin reported that the settled at mediation, that Ms. Cazahous will buy out the interest of Philip leaving 2 beneficiaries, that the proposed settlement calls for the parties to make a motion to seal the record, that there is pending arbitration between Mr. Patel and Ms. Cazahous regarding the building, that the matter has been submitted to JAMS for binding arbitration, that the dates have been put out until November, that the issue is whether there is still an enforceable agreement, that there is a schedule for depositions to be taken, that Mr. Sirot is present today because Mr. Patel has concerns regarding the settlement, that the trust maintains full ownership of the hotel and that there are concerns regarding the documents that will be requested to be sealed. Mr. Mraule stated that they will withdraw their petition for instructions, that the matter settled some time ago and it is unfortunate that they are incurring additional expenses regarding this objection. Mr. Sirot stated that they are still concerned regarding the effect of settlement on the property, that the order submitted did not address the property by address, that the time constraints regarding the motion to seal pushed them to file the objections and that they will meet and confer regarding the sealing of the documents. The Court took the petition for approval of settlement agreement and the accounting petition under submission. The status on sale of the property and the petition for instructions regarding subtrust funding, etc., are off calendar.

Judge: Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-08-2015 Continued To Jun-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Sale Of The Property
Off Calendar Apr-08-2015 Continued To Jun-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mraule for and with (petitioner & objector) Philip Cazahous. William Balin & Nicole Kelly for and with (trustee) Ann Cazahous.

Mr. Balin reported that the attachments to the petition had been filed yesterday, that notice had been given at the last hearing date, that notice was waived, that the sale of the property is in limbo, that the buyer is litigating, that the buyer filed 2 Lis Pendens and they are waiting to see the order which was signed yesterday and that they are going to arbitration (regarding the property). Mr. Mraule reported that they have not received the monthly updates as per the stipulation, that the accounting petition was filed late, reiterated their request to have the trustee suspended or to appoint a co-trustee to oversee what is being done and stated his concerns with the accounting showing transfers to non trust accounts, credit card use and cash withdrawals. Mr. Balin stated that emails had been sent as a form of update, that some cash assets & stocks had been distributed previously, that they had discovered a number of accounts no previously known, that not all accounts were to go into the trust, and that bank records had been subpoenaed in late December and the last documents were received on March 17, 2015 and that previous to this date they did not have all of the documents needed to produce a complete accounting. The court stated that the trustee has not been timely in filing requests from the court and asked if the parties were amenable to mediation, stating that there a lot of issues between the two parties to work out. Mr. Balin stated that the Broadway property is the only one left and it is currently in litigation, but is open to mediation. The status on sale of the property, the accounting petition and the petition for instructions regarding subtrust funding, etc., were continued to June 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties were told to mediate prior to this date.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Dec-31-2014 Continued To Mar-11-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mraule for (Beneficiary) Philip Cazahous. William Balin for and with (Trustee) Ann Cazahous.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the trustee having not complied with the court order to provide information to the beneficiaries, whether or not a continuance for amendment to the accounting should be allowed, whether or not removal of the trustee and appointment of Mr. Mraule's client or a professional trustee is appropriate at this time, whether the parties are willing/ready to participate in mediation, etc. Mr. Balin stated that he anticipates receiving bank statements within 30 days, that he can have the amended accounting filed by March 3, 2015, that estate taxes were filed in July and that he will continue to provide information regarding the property. Mr. Balin also clarified that he will file an association of counsel and that he is representing Ms. Cazahous as trustee. All matters were continued to March 11, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court wants counsel to explore/have conversation with their clients regarding mediation.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Accounting Petition.
Off Calendar Sep-17-2014 Continued To Oct-01-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mraule for and with Philip Cazahous (Petitioner). Francis Kelly and Nicole Kelly for and with Ann Cazahous (Respondent).

Mr. Mraule stated that they don't have an accounting and asked the Court to remove Ms. Cazahous and appoint his Client. Mr. Kelly stated that he will have the accounting filed by Friday. The Court reminded Mr. Kelly that he needs a petition for approval of the accounting under Probate Code 1060. The Parties addressed the issue of whether or not tax documents/K-1's had been prepared/provided. The Court encouraged the Parties to cooperate. Status on filing of the accounting and the petition for instructions were continued to October 1, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting is to be filed by September 19, 2014.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petition For Instructions Regarding Subtrust Funding And Order Compelling Report And Accounting
Off Calendar Sep-17-2014 Continued To Oct-01-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Regarding Subtrust Funding And Order Compelling Report And Accounting
Off Calendar Aug-06-2014 Continued To Sep-17-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Matthew Mraule for and with (petitioner) Philip Cazahous. Francis Kelly for and with (respondent) Ann Cazahous. Nicole Kelly also present.

Mr. Kelly stated that the Broadway property is in contract, that a lis pendens was filed, that he was told that something was wrong with title and explained how the property passed to the trust. Mr. Mraule stated that they were unaware that the property is in escrow, that the property is under valued by $500,000.00. The parties were asked to step out of the courtroom and discuss the matter. Upon returning Mr. Mraule indicated that they agreed to withdraw the lis pendens, that his client will provide a list of questions regarding the Broadway property not to exceed 20 questions, which will be due in 14 days, that monthly status report regarding sale is to be provided, that a status hearing is to be held within 2-3 months out regarding estimated closing and that the gross proceeds be deposited into a blocked account. Mr. Mraule is to reduce the above to a stipulation and order. The court ordered an accounting for the period of April 8, 2013 to July 31, 2014 in format and petition for approval under probate code 1060, status on filing of the accounting was set for September 17, 2014. The court declined Mr. Mraule's request to suspend Ann Cazahous today and encouraged the parties to try and get the property sold. The issue of basis numbers being incorrect was also raised. The court will not make an order on Mr. Mraule's request to not allow the trustee to spend any trust funds. The petition for instructions was continued to September 17, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
